note: First end-to-end percent-of-oracle numbers on real Claude Code traces (kv-cache-tester), 2026-07-21. The two regimes together are the honest frame for the eventual results table: the control shows the benchmark does not manufacture wins where theory says none; the pressure point shows the headroom lifecycle policies must earn.

low_pressure_control.reading: LRU near-optimal, as the pre-registered eval predicts for the regime where policy should not matter (Scenario-5 property).

high_pressure.reading: LRU pays 60% more than the offline optimum under real pressure; this is the headroom lifecycle-aware policies must convert into wins.

granularity_sensitivity.conclusion: policy comparison is granularity-invariant; the sweep runs at 256 (EVAL_PLAN sweep grid).

performance_decision.first_order_lever: block-granularity coarsening 64 -> 256 (a simulation parameter, not an engine constraint)
performance_decision.result: full 11-session interleave went from intractable at 64 to 28.8s at 256
performance_decision.grid_arithmetic: ~29s/cell x ~144 cells = ~69 min for the full sweep
performance_decision.conclusion: coarsening alone is sufficient; the heap-based O(resident) eviction optimization is NOT needed and is not being built. Cheap lever measured, expensive lever declined.
